name: Lily
communication_level: highly verbal with advanced vocabulary; monotone delivery and literal interpretation; struggles with sarcasm, idioms, and reading nonverbal cues; tends to monologue about marine life
interests: marine biology (especially octopuses  -  knows all 300+ species)
collecting and sorting rocks by color, weight, and type
color-sorting anything (crayons, beads, books on shelves)

seeks: deep pressure (bear hugs, compression vest, weighted blanket)
weighted items (lap pad, heavy books to carry)
tactile input from smooth rocks and cool surfaces
avoids: strong smells (hand sanitizer, markers, cafeteria cooking)
fluorescent light flicker (prefers natural light or lamp)
calming_strategies: quiet corner with noise-reducing headphones and rock collection

reinforcers: 5 minutes to read DK Oceans encyclopedia
adding a new rock to her classroom collection

iep_goals.description: Lily will demonstrate pragmatic language skills by (a) staying on a conversational topic chosen by a peer for at least 3 exchanges, and (b) identifying a peer's emotional state from facial/body cues in 8 out of 10 structured opportunities, as measured by SLP observation and social skills group data.
iep_goals.measurement_method: SLP observation during social skills group and classroom interactions

trial_history.notes: Stayed on peer's topic (soccer) for 2 exchanges before pivoting to octopuses. Identified 'happy' and 'sad' faces but missed 'frustrated' and 'nervous.'
Using the 'topic tracker' visual card  -  helped her notice when she's drifting. Correctly identified 6/10 emotions in photo cards.
Had a real conversation with Mia about their pets  -  4 exchanges on topic! Still struggles in groups larger than 2. Emotion recognition improving with the 'body clue' checklist.
Consistent in 1:1 conversations. Group settings still hard  -  she goes quiet or monologues. Noticed peer was upset at recess and said 'are you okay?' unprompted. Big moment.
Strong week. Topic maintenance at 4+ exchanges in 1:1. During group project, asked a peer 'what do you think?'  -  first time she's invited input.
Nearing target. Read peer's frustration during math partner work and offered help. SLP notes she's internalizing the body clue checklist. Group conversations still need support.

iep_goals.description: Lily will independently initiate and complete assigned classroom tasks (including multi-step projects) within the allotted time in 7 out of 10 opportunities, using a visual checklist if needed, as measured by teacher task completion log.
iep_goals.measurement_method: teacher task completion log with timestamps

trial_history.notes: Initiates preferred tasks (reading, sorting) quickly. Avoids open-ended writing  -  stares at paper for 10+ minutes. Visual checklist helps once she starts.
Regression. Science fair project assigned  -  multi-step with ambiguous instructions overwhelmed her. Shut down for 20 minutes. Need to pre-chunk unfamiliar projects.
Still recovering from science fair stress. Refusing to start anything that 'doesn't have clear steps.' Added pre-task planning sheet where she writes her own checklist before starting.
Pre-task planning sheet working  -  she writes detailed checklists. Initiated the ocean research project within 2 minutes (high interest). Math worksheet still takes 8 minutes to begin.
Slight uptick. Using a timer now  -  she sets it herself for 'start by' time. Completed 6/10 tasks within time. The 4 missed were all multi-step with unclear endpoints.
Continuing recovery. Self-checklist becoming habitual for familiar tasks. Still needs teacher support to break down novel multi-step assignments. Progress is real but slow.

iep_goals.description: Lily will use a learned coping strategy (deep breathing, requesting a break, using her worry stone, or asking for help) instead of crying or shutting down when frustrated during group work, in 7 out of 10 frustrating situations, as measured by behavior tracking log.
iep_goals.measurement_method: behavior tracking log  -  coping strategy used vs. shutdown/crying

trial_history.notes: Group math activity  -  cried when peer disagreed with her answer. Used worry stone afterward but not before the meltdown. Needs anticipatory cue.
Introduced '5-point scale' rating card. Lily identifies she's at a 3 before it reaches 5. Used break card twice this week before shutting down.
Science fair group work was rough but she asked for a break instead of crying  -  first time in a group setting. Peer conflict at lunch still triggers immediate tears.
Using worry stone proactively during group transitions. Requested to work alone on one task  -  appropriate self-advocacy rather than shutdown.
Big week. During a disagreement about the group poster, Lily said 'I need a minute' and walked to the calm corner. Came back and compromised. Teacher almost cried.

Consistent use of strategies in familiar settings. Used deep breathing during a timed math test without prompting. Cafeteria meltdown on Thursday after spilled milk (smell trigger)  -  not counted as group work but worth noting.
